title: Iran 1956/1966 census results and Pahlavi-era vital statistics, extracted from already-downloaded World Bank archival PDFs (1962, 1971, 1974 reports)
source_org: World Bank (IBRD), citing Statistical Centre of Iran / Ministry of Interior censuses, Plan Organization, Plan and Budget Organization, Bank Markazi Iran
extraction_method: The underlying PDFs (1962_economic_development_program.pdf, 1971_cep_vol3_population_employment_family_planning.pdf, 1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf, 1974_economic_development_vol4_statistical_appendix.pdf) were already present in ../historical-documents/ from a prior download pass and had never been text-extracted. Ran pdftotext -layout on each, then manually transcribed every year-tagged population/demographic/labor-force statistic explicitly stated in the prose or appearing in a titled table, following the project's iranica-fiscal-system-narrative-series precedent (data.csv: year_or_period, metric, value, unit, cited_source with PDF filename + paragraph or table number). OCR quality varies a lot across these 1960s/70s-vintage scanned documents -- text is often corrupted (e.g. 'aind' for 'and', digit transpositions). Values that were too corrupted to read with confidence were EXCLUDED rather than guessed; values with minor OCR uncertainty are marked with '~' or an explicit note. No number was calculated, interpolated, or estimated beyond what the source itself states.
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription from already-downloaded, newly pdftotext-extracted PDF text)
time_coverage: 1956-1982 (mostly 1956, 1960, 1962, 1966, 1967-1972 intercensal, and 1982 Plan Organization projections made in 1971)
frequency: irregular -- census years, sample-survey years, and whatever periodization the source WB reports used
license_terms: World Bank Documents & Reports -- public, no explicit reuse restriction stated on individual pre-2003 report PDFs.
notes: MISSION CONTEXT: this extraction directly addresses the confirmed gap that Iran's 1956/1966/1976 census original publications are not directly downloadable (see ../../iran-census/iran-census-historical/manifest.json for that confirmation). These World Bank reports were written close in time to the censuses (1962 report written 6 years after the 1956 census; 1971/1974 reports written 5-8 years after the 1966 census) and CITE specific census-derived figures in their own prose/tables, making them a legitimate secondary citation source per this project's stated methodology (cite the dated, page/paragraph-referenced figure as a row). KEY CROSS-VALIDATION: the 1962 report's transcription of the 1956 census total (18,954,704, para 7 p.131) matches EXACTLY (to the last digit) the clean figure independently obtained from the UN Demographic Yearbook Historical Supplement Table 2 (see ../../un-demographic-yearbook-historical/) -- strong confirmation that both sources are citing the same original SCI/Ministry of Interior definitive count, and that the WB OCR-garbled version of this number (which appeared as '18, 954,1,040' in raw pdftotext output) can be trusted as 18,954,704 once cross-checked. KNOWN DISCREPANCIES flagged in data.csv rather than resolved: (1) two different life-expectancy-in-1966 figures appear within the SAME 1971 WB document (53 years in narrative para 4 vs 45.6 years in the Plan-Organization-sourced Table 1.4) -- the document is internally inconsistent, both values recorded; (2) the WB's own SCI-derived under-enumeration adjustment factors (~2% for 1956, ~1% for 1966, per the 1974 report's Technical Note) are markedly lower than Julian Bharier's independent 1968 academic estimate (7.5% for 1956, 5.0% for 1966, 2.5% for 1976) cited in the Encyclopaedia Iranica DEMOGRAPHY article (see ../../iran-census/iranica-census-demography-narrative-series-1868-1998/) -- recorded as a genuine range per this project's bias-policy on contested estimates, not resolved to a single number. The provincial table (Table 1.2 of the 1974 report) has one OCR-uncertain province name (row 18, flagged explicitly in that CSV's note column) and several 1956-census values that appear bracketed/shared between two adjacent provinces in the original table's typesetting (also flagged per-row) -- these are logged as-is rather than guess-corrected.
